reviews.review_msg: I took an undergrad Crim course with Dr. Verdun-Jones at SFU in 1976. From these reviews, it doesn't look like he's lost his engaging delivery style, intellect, or passion. (Gained a cane, though!)
Low-key failed his course, his lectures are hella tight though
Amazing, funny guy. He uses a cane to walk and its cool to see him walking around while yelling about crime. Hes awesome! Can't wait to have him again!
Best prof at SFU, lectures are interesting and the tutorials are helpful.

Simon is an amazing prof! He has so much passion when he lectures, would recommend you don't sit at the very front because he spits a lot! Exams are straightforward study the courseware and read the text you'll be fine if you put in the work Take the tutorial with Marsha-Ann, she the real MVP
Simon is the worst professor ever I met in sfu

Best prof I've ever had in SFU. Super interesting lectures. Encourages you to not take notes and just listen to him. He talks in detail about real-life cases, and then you read on your own about all the theory. Not hard at all if you put in the work!

Dr. Verdun-Jones is easily one of the best professors at SFU! His lectures are crystal clear, entertaining, and engaging. I managed an A+ in the course barely reading the textbook because he goes over everything so well. The custom courseware will save your life! Midterm not bad, final was more difficult with the scenario cases. Would recommend!!
Amazing lecturer, definitely go to class because he makes it way more specific. Make sure you do the required readings as well. Overall a really great class and professor.
Very interesting lectures and overall a fair marker

Simon is an amazing lecturer. His lectures are really explanatory and allows students to really understand the major concepts. Read his textbooks, but the coursebook, and the case book. They are LIFESAVERS!! Lectures will make much more sense if you actually take time to read the textbooks. Also, Open clinic for Finals really helped! Recommend this

His final exam was so hard, but that was partially my fault. His class is clear and fair. Honestly, your experience depends on your TA. I interacted with him twice throughout the whole term. You don't need to read the Criminal Law textbook if you pay good attention in lectures - they're basically the same. Custom courseware  lifesaver.

Love this dude. Though he accidentally spits sometimes, its cus he's so passionate when he talks. Knows his material REALLY well. Exams can be tricky, but if you know your material, you're good to go. Go to every class. He should teach more classes.
One of the most entertaining professors I've had - very animated and engaging during lectures which made going to class fun!
Great prof! Uses lots of memorable and humourous examples to explain concepts. Lectures and readings all complimented one another really well, which made it easier to grasp difficult topics.

Awesome prof! I did take it online and there is not much work needed. Keep up with the readings in the textbook to help you do well on the final. The TM was awesome for the course, extremely helpful and fair grader. Did not need to memorize prev cases for the online class anyway!

He does not post slides online or have lecture recordings available. However, the CCW is all you need to succeed in this course. I attended lectures and used the CCW exclusively for studying for exams, and did not read the textbook, and managed an A in this course without too much effort. Overall a fun and easy class.
The professor genuinely cares about the students. He offered a bundle of examples which really helped with the notes. He invests enthusiasm  which makes the class engaging! Overall, he's great!

He is so enthusiastic during the lectures. Gives a lot of appropriate case examples relevant to the topic that makes it more easy to understand the course material. The exams are pretty fair. If you attend his lectures and as long as you read the case readings, you are good to go.!

Interesting and enjoyable course material if you are interested in criminal law. Many interesting cases. Prof's lecture is informative and helpful but powerpoint slides are a little distracting.. hard to follow.

He's a great speaker that always manages to hook your interest and keep you hooked. With all of the examples he uses, there's almost no way you can get stuck on a certain concept; he's easy to approach if you do get stuck, however. Might not want to sit at the very front though because he's very loud, even without the mic.

Dr. Verdun-Jones is an amazing prof.  His lectures are exciting, his use of power point slides is excellent, and his explanations are both useful and interesting.  I would defiantly try to get into one of his classes again.

I took Crim 230 with Simon a few years back. He is undoubtedly one of the most knowledgeable profs in the Crim department. While the material is quite dry, he illuminates it well with relevant cases that help you understand the material immensely. Don't sit up front though, he has the tendency to yell unexpectedly haha.
Very accomplished, very interesting.
Amazing prof! Makes class super interesting and the textbook is easy to follow. Loved his class!
Awesome prof, the best there is in criminology. Very clear and makes lectures very interesting!

great professor!! keep lectures very interesting and uses a lot of cases to signify his point. the course material is heavy and requires you to remember 12+ cases for the exams, but honestly the exams are not hard. if you go to class and do the textbook readings, you'll do great. an A is doable. highly recommended!!!

GO TO CLASS - He is hilarious and makes Crim super fun! Sit in the back he is pretty loud. Do the reading and go to class and you will understand the material without a doubt, he is great at explaining things.
Very good. is your planning to be a lawyer or anything related take him. But remember to cite your cases!!. he is a great prof.

Best Overall professor i have had so far! Not a super easy class, but it is challenging and you will learn a lot. He is really passionate about the material, and knows it like the back of his hand.  HIGHLY recommended. GREAT prof.

Almost 4th year, one of the best profs I have ever had thus far! He is absolutely knowledgeable and highly qualified for the subjects he teaches/talks about. Great examples, very animated and engaged in class discussion/very passionate about topics and course over all. Exams-Very good. Midterm EASY, Final requires higher level of topic understandin

What a great prof! Really made this class interesting. Fair exams. The only really difficult part of the course was the fact pattern question in the final....brutal! But, on a whole I really enjoyed this class.
I liked his lecture and he is a great prof. I also liked his books as well -- very helpful and comprehensible. Using examples always helped me to understand the concepts.
I love his class. He is so much fun! He is interesting and entertaining!
He is a very good teacher, very fair.

one of the best profs that ive had. makes it really interesting and uses lots of examples to make everyone understand. go to the clinics before the exams! very helpful and attend tutorials the tas go over everything you need to know.

You cannot leave SFU with a Crim degree without taking his class. He is one of the best prof and so far, one of my fav. The course is not easy and you really need to work hard for it but it's extremely interesting. The readings aren't so bad either... the exams are fair. (Typical crim exam) The case was a bit hard though (final).

You will NEVER fall asleep in this class, Dr.Jones is very loud and very fun!! good, fair marker, makes fair exams, but you HAVE to go to the clinic before the exam cuz he covers EXACTLY wuts gonna be on the exam and what the exam is gonna look like. you dont have to attent classes, but do go to all of the TUT since the TA's cover everyhting

The class moves at a very fast pace but the materials are clearly covered in class. For most parts, students will find themselves time crunched for multiple choice and short answers questions and he does mark very hard (tell Theresa  the TA marks very hard) I would take him again, as he is a very good to teacher.

MUST attend lectures and MUST read ALL the textbooks if you want to do well. Textbooks are lengthy but they are all required readings. Must attend lecture and listen to him as he explains the concepts in more depth.  Interesting and very useful course. Exams are fair. Dr. Verdun-Jones talks very clearly and he is nice and friendly.
Final exam was hard. Tells you which concepts are important but there is too much to know. He has a passion for the course but expectations for final are somewhat unrealistic.
One of the best CRIM profs for sure!  Very passionate about teaching..lectures are interesting..exams are fair

This is an extremely useful and interesting class! And I agree with everyone before me that this is a class where you must work hard for your grades. Just make sure you do the readings each week before you attend the class and you'll do fine. Michelle may speak too fast sometimes, but she is willing to clarify it again for you after the tutorial.
Cool, but hard.

I love how the person 2 comments down wrote two bad comments just to make Simon's mark go down.  Simon is a great prof that requires you to work for your mark.  It's a really neat and fun course otherwise!  If you do not put in the effort, you will not do well.  Maybe this is why Simon got a bad mark?
Course material was fair. Lots of memorization but the exams werent too difficult. Just follow the lecture and take good notes and suppliment with text.

Misleading exam criteria, they say they want one thing before the exam but after the exam they change the criteria and give you a really low mark. TA's unreasonable and does not stick by her word. I wouldn't recommend this class. There is too memorization and the textbook is very wordy. Overall, this course is difficult.

Do not recommend. Verdon-Jones talks very loud during lectures and it is hard to stay awake. If you enjoy getting low marks and endless memorization to a 'T', then take this course. However, if you want an A, this is not a course for you. Exams are hard.

Simon is a great lecturer, however, his TA Michelle is not. Mid-term is very difficult and we are told to write 2 sentences for the short answer, but once marked, she clearly wants more. Hard to get a good grade in the course as there are no half marks awarded on exams. Michelle is a nervous wreck. Not a GPA booster by any means.

This is a tough course. Michelle L. is the TA for the course and marks everything extremely hard and she gives no half marks, so you have to know everything in full, which is stupid. When you go and ask her why you did bad on some questions she just becomes excited and anxious and does not care about why you did not get 2/2 on the short answers.
Not easy, but good course

Amazing teacher and truly loves his work. Don't think this class is a joke. He explains things really well but you do have to put some effort in to get the A. Good course though and recommended. Not an easy GPA booster, but definitely not hard either.

Great prof. he is a genius which makes you intimidated sometimes. he knows the topic and details of every case he talks about! the subject is clear to him, so everything makes sense when he explains it, but sometimes they become confusing again at home! easy to get a good mark if you actually do some work

He is a great prof. He talks fast which can make it hard to follow but if you read the text you should be fine because he wrote them & they relate to lecture. Michelle is an awesome TA. She is a lawyer so she really knows her stuff. The midterm m/c were tricky because if there are two answers you must state both and no half marks are given.

So far Simon-Verdun Jones has been my favorite prof at SFU.  He is simply phenomenal!  A very enthusiastic and brilliant guy to listen to in lectures.  I feel bad for the people who took this course Distance Ed - you're missing out!
AWESOME AWESOME prof. The best prof you will get! Knows his material, exciting lectures, useful.... Just Love this course. I'd take it again and again! =D

Passionate about his teaching the law. Very assertive voice and easy to listen to in lecture. You can tell that this man was a Crown Prosector. I liked his lectures. but make sure u attend tutorials or u will not do well.

Took it by distance ed and was a little reluctant due to the course contant.  But the texts were easy to understand and apply to the essays.  Assignments were straightforward adn the TA were very helpful.  Study the last few units for the final and you will be fine.  Got an A- and only opened the books when writing the assignments.  Learned a lot!
No doubt the best prof I've ever had. I love you Dr. Verdun-Jones
Dr. Simon Verdun-Jones is the BEST criminology professor at SFU! He is an excellent lecturer, and he is so passionate about his teachings. SIMON ROCKS!!!

He is awesome. He is very friendly, enthusiastic and engaging. Gotta love the names he uses in his textbook case examples. Make sure you do your readings. If you do, there shouldn't be any surprises when you write your midterm. Standout guy.
No Comments

this man DEFINATELY knows his stuff... very knowledgable. gets really into his lectures. tends to talk loudly at times, but it keeps you awake. essay and exams are fair. lectures are interesting. good prof!

V-J is very intelligent, can keep you interested for a three hour seminar, and provides outlines/materials that you will need to study with.  He makes all case examples clear and concise, and seems like he loves teaching. Be sure to take one of his classes before you grad!
distance ed. dont know much abt the prof, but the course was interesting, although the book was not lol
Great Prof; knows the cirriculum very well and he's interested in the course, so it makes it fun because he knows exact details about every case he talks about. It's an awesome class!

He is an amazing prof.  If you don't like criminology, Simon will change that.  He is brilliant and very easy to communicate with.  Exams are tough but very fair.  I would highly recommend any class that he is teaching!  10+++++++++
Verdun-Jones is so passionate about his teaching. His enthusiasm and energy really gets you into the class. Amazing professor and amazing course. Interesting content.

he's definitely knows his stuff, it was a pleasure to be in his class.  He's the reason why we should be proud of the criminology department.  he's the type of prof that you would admire because he's so intelligent that he's almost bordline insane.
Excellent, intense, knows his stuff. Take his course and you'll never "actus reus" again!
knows his stuff to the bone. Is interesting, made me more interested in criminal law.  Very down to earth.
Great notes and lectures; but, tough.  Study for the final exam.

I dont know what these people are talking about, the course material (written by Simon) is garbage.  For instance, the chapter about actus reus only talks about mens rea.  He contradicts himself constantly, and runs the crim. department terribly.
Great prof, but virtually lost my hearing. Warning: do not sit in first 2 rows as when he gets really excited he tends to spray as he shouts.

Prof Verdun-Jones is so into the course material it's scary.  Incredible energy and enthusiasm.  I would definately take a class with him again.  As long as you do all the reading, this class is easy.  Very clear on test material.

Amazing professor.  He knows so much about everything - it's unbelievable.  Follows order of CCW, so lectures are very easy to follow and keep up.  Talks really loud - doesn't matter where you sit, you can hear him very clearly!
took it by distance education, great books!
Took Crim 230 by Dist. Ed.  Very helpful.  Willing to spend time on phone.  I found him very soft spoken,  Down to earth.
he talks REALLY LOUDLY.  bring earplugs if you sit near the front.  your ears will hurt otherwise.
Brilliant, engaging, eccentric.
he gets really into the lectures. very organized. great prof.
he is nice
No Comments
very engagin, entertaining, knowledgeable
Such a good prof, he really makes what could be super hard material easy to understand! Makes lectures interesting & easy to follow!
This course is not easy and requires a lot of work but Pete makes this course very enjoyable.

PRetty tough course but if you put your time into it you can manage at least a B. As far as Pete goes, he Is the best professor ive taken so far at sfu. Brilliant professor who keeps class very interesting with his stories especially the ones from wrestling. Id come to this class pretty hung over and i managed a B, but i studied hard. SICK GUY!!

Awesome prof. Very clear explanations and keeps a difficult course interesting. Bring a laptop to lecture if you can because it is NONSTOP notes. He's also super cool and tells funny stories every class. Definitely recommend!

THIS IS THE COOLEST PERSON YOU WILL EVER MEET... hes super nice and makes a difficult course fathomable. I encourage anyone who has the opportunity to take a class with him to take it. The man is super smart, chill, and inspiring. this is the man

Pete is the best teacher I've had at SFU. He is passionate about the material and he incorporates entertaining examples to support his teachings. His notes are incredible...o and he allows a cheat sheet for the midterm/final. It is a super tough course but incredibly interesting!!!
Best. Prof. Ever. He makes criminal law so interesting. A lot of note taking, but totally worth it.
Very knowledgeable and well versed in criminal law. His lectures are 2 hours of straight note taking. Learned alot from him this semester. His exams are quite difficult and time constrained.

Very clear and precise. LOTS of notes and lots of memorization, but it's fairly interesting. He gives lots of example cases to help. Midterm only counts if you do worse on the final. No papers, just two exams and tutorial.

Fall 2021 he would not record lectures, this made it difficult to keep up cuz the lectures are long and dense. The tutorial participation/project are easy to get a good grade on. The memos take a lot of time but are fairly straight forward if you have good lecture notes. The final is ridiculous, it is so insanely long and you will run out of time

Dr. Fehr has an incredibly impressive background and his lectures were even better because of that. He was able to bring the material to life with real examples and his experiences as a lawyer. The course material itself can be quite dense and at times hard to grasp but going to office hours seriously makes all the difference in this class.

THIS PROF IS BY FAR THE WORST PROF I'VE EVER HAD. What he expects from students is ridiculously unrealistic and the amount of time given for exams makes it nearly impossible to finish. I genuinely do not understand how he has any good ratings. DO NOT take this class with him ABORT AT ALL COSTS!!!

The course in general was okay. However, the final exam was beyond unreasonable. For the two previous assignments we were given weeks notice to work on it. For the final exam that contained nearly twice the amount of work we only had 3 hrs plus an essay. Felt impossible to finish.

Had him online for Fall 2020, lectures were mandatory because he didn't do recordings to encourage attendance, posts slides before the lecture.. But his lectures are really good at summarizing the textbook material and cites cases that will help you for the memos, as well he will give hints on what cases/statutes to focus on for memos/final.

Colton is extremely knowledgeable at his subject, which allows a very in-depth course. The only problem that I found was that sometimes it is hard to catch everything he says, as he sometimes goes over topics quickly if nobody has questions. I would suggest having the pp notes ready beforehand and just add the extra things he mentions.

Professor Fehr is extremely knowledgeable and truly cares about his students. Attending his office hours is helpful as he is more than willing to point you in the right direction. If youre passionate about the law and constitutional issues, you will enjoy Fehrs engaging and thought-provoking lectures.

It was his first time teaching at SFU and he honestly taught very well and was resourceful and accessible. Content was heavy but he explained it well in lectures and gave examples. Pay attention to lectures because he will give clues about assignments and exams. Listen to every detail and focus on every point in the criteria and go to office hours!

Tamara is so kind and respectful, really putting in the effort to support all students through extensions, advice, and feedback. Assignments were fun and engaging, and I genuinely learned so much in this course through the lectures, assignments, and readings. Overall very fun, 10/10 professor, would take a class with her again

Course is challenging, due to the extensive volume of cases and legal terms. Can feel overwhelming given the amount of material. However Tamara played a crucial role in making these concepts more accessible. She breaks down difficult topics into digestible, manageable portions, and making the material reletable. I highly recommend Tamara as a prof

She barely does any teaching, its all reading so get ready to read the textbook in detail yourself. Its super heavy material and there is only class when she's doing an assessment which is every other week...she doesn't do anything to bring the material together and you're stuck reading loads of legal information and trying to make sense of it

Tamara was a fantastic professor! She's very accommodating if have a disability. You can really tell she cares about the success of her students. She is enthusiastic and passionate about what she teaches so her lectures are engaging. Feedback from the TA is helpful. Fun class if you like law. 10/10 for me.

Dr. O'Doherty was amazing! She made the class fun and engaging with weekly highlights lectures to make sure you understand the material. Also, the assignments are straightforward and easy to do well on if you take the time to do it. Overall amazing class and a great professor. Definitely recommend taking this class with her!

Class was 'self directed' so what is the point of paying so much? Lectures were 15 mins long and exams were form book and nothing form what she posts. Memo 1 was unclear, lost majority of points there. Expects so much and teaches so less, the most difficult class ever! I never want to study such a course again, I would rather change my major.

Amazing professor who lays out everything that is expected clearly. Exams were open book and Q&A sessions were held before all exams and assignments. Also offered a syllabus quiz where, if taken, allowed a 48 hour extension on any assignment

Dr. O'Doherty was absent outside of zoom calls. Grading was clear, but assignment instructions were difficult to understand. Wrote my first memo and had no idea what to do, minimal explanation. TA's didn't knock marks for it being your first ever memo though. Exams were easy, take your own notes and prepare! Exam 3hrs long, in sections!
Tamara was a very kind professor who made the assignments easy to follow. The class had long chapters to read each week, but weekly work was minimal. Very interesting and comprehensive course.

I loved her, she is so nice and helpful. You can tell she wants her students to do well. Keep up with the readings. Her lecture videos were helpful and the assignments were not hard. I hope I get to take her classes again.

Took CRIM 230 online and was really scared when looking at the marks breakdown, but, upon actually taking the class Tamara made the class easy to follow and was very helpful if you emailed about an assignment or wanted clarification was very accessible. Tamara holds office hours prior to major grading components (exams and assignments).
No lectures, and unclear guidelines.
Doesn't post any lectures so you have to self teach. Horrible exams; so difficult! (I did really well in 135 too). Would not recommend unless you want your gpa to drop.

Tamara is the most knowledgeable and kind professor we have in the crim department. Not only is she an amazing professor, but she teaches the lecture material in a way that is easy to grasp and helps you learn to have a critical eye. Her classes are not easy per say, but if you put in the work, you will be rewarded.
Pretty Hard for a second year course. The TA saved all the students from failing. Needed to use entire time for exams.

I took crim230 online and luckily, I had an amazing TM who made the class super enjoyable. The modules were really organized and made studying so easy. The midterm is take-home and the assignments help you prepare for the final. Study the cases that are outlined in the modules to do well on the final! Super fun class to take online.
Went way to fast over slides so you cannot get anything down, does not post slides.
LOVE HER

Definitely one of my favourite professors. She does fly through lecture slides at a faster than average pace, but if you pay attention it really isn't a problem (plus she posts all her lectures in canvas beforehand). Keeps her lectures interesting and links material to current events. The class is definitely tough and heavy on readings, but do-able
Great prof, amazing lectures, exams were fair but get ready to read!

She's an engaging prof, but if you're just looking for an easy A, not the prof to take. She goes through slides way too fast, but does touch on interesting topics which is always a good discussion. I probably wouldn't take her again because of her exams, they're super hard, but she herself is a sweet person.
she nice! i like!

Karm is a stellar teaching assistant; she makes every effort possible to help her students, and her warm personality is very comforting when you find yourself lost in the material. Further to this, her tutorials are informative, and she does a great job of engaging the class without singling anybody out.
She was really nice and very knowledgeable about the topic.

The low marks are people who don't follow her instructions or do the readings. Not a hard class if you do readings, take good notes and engage with the content. By the final I had tons memorized naturally from lecture. She gives feedback from assignments & a cheat sheet for the exam! One of my fav classes as a crim major, and one of my fav profs!

I have not experienced such a hard marking professor like Amy Conroy. Her lectures can be very confusing. When she tries to explain the concepts, she often doesn't make her point clear. When she answers students questions, her answers aren't clear. All exams, especially MC was confusing. Avoid taking her! Better off memorizing the textbook.

Dr. Conroy is a very good professor, she cares about her students and always rounds grades up at the end of the semester. There is a lot of reading for her courses but as long as you work to have a good foundation of the concepts you will do well.

Amy Conroy's 230 crim class was one of the most confusing courses I have ever taken. If you don't ready every page of the textbook you will fail her exams. most of the material on the midterm and final was in confusing hard to understand language. 3 out 4 options on multiple choice Q's would be saying the same thing in different ways.
She barely teaches, all u do is just read from the textbook. She is the worst prof ive ever had.
WHO IS RATING THIS WOMAN POSITIVELY
oh. my. god. she is extremely unproffessional

This is my 2nd class with Amy. The bad ratings are from people who don't care to study properly. Read the textbook and take thorough notes on it, come to class prepared, and you can do good. It's def a lot of reading (this is a law class after all) but you get a cheat sheet for tests thank god. She's a great prof, it's too bad she has low ratings.

I have taken hella Crim Courses. So far, the ones with Amy have been the most difficult. Spends more time talking about how to study etc, instead of actually teaching course material.
Every exam question feels like a trick question, or related to something mentioned ONCE, rather than significant textbook topic. (3.5gpa in crim her classes are buns)

Honestly, these reviews scared me but I didn't find her to be too bad. I found the canvas a bit confusing since you had to find everything through modules making it hard to go back and forth. Gives cheat sheets for tests, and when you think you have enough put more. It saved me.

attend lecture and do the readings and you will be okay. I found it pretty evident which cases were going to be on the exams as they were either talked about at length in the textbook (and brought up many times) or she talked about it in lecture. grading was okay, I must have done well on the final to get the grade I did

Do the readings and go to lecture and you'll be totally fine. She puts stuff from both the readings and lecture on the midterm and final. If you miss lecture you won't be able to get a good grade on either the final or the midterm. Basically do what you're supposed to and you'll do good.

Amy is a pretty good prof, yes there is a lot to read but she is clear on her expectations. the grading is not insanely harsh and she provides general feedback on how/why you might have done bad and how you can improve. if you want personal feedback she has office hours

Conroy is by far the worst prof I've had at SFU she was unorganized, often late, and so unapproachable. Asking for help or feedback always resulted in "did you read the course outline", "did you read the textbook", yes I did lady you're just confusing as hell. Her grading criteria's are very confusing and she does not state everything she wants. NO

This is a LAW class; it will be difficult: lots of reading & complex material. If you study, read the textbook, follow instructions, and take your time, it's easy. The class focuses on applying the law; you need to be thorough with your assignments. Amy isn't targeting you, she's lovely who wants students to do well but you need to put in the work.
I didn't even do that bad and I'm warning you please do not take this class with her. Save yourself. Take it with literally anyone else.

the class is tough but if you actually do the readings and don't just ask AI for the answers you will do pretty well. people complaining about how the cheat sheet doesn't help either aren't making it the way she literally tells you to or are just using ai to make it/to study. hard class but if you put in the work and listen to her advice....

she expects us to memorize so much but her lectures mainly consist of her talking about specific cases or things she finds interesting that are not too closely related to the remainder of the course material. they are definitely interesting though! her slides also mainly consist of excerpts from the textbook which doesn't help us grasp content

i actually like amy! she's definitely tough, and yes you have to read a lot, but i actually learn in her classes. as long as you actually put in effort and do not use chat gpt for your study cheats, you'll do fine. she's just a teacher you actually makes you put in effort. if crim is something that interests you, you will like her.

Not an easy class, but these comments aren't fair. She was irritated after our midterm because so many students studied with AI and were misinformed. That is why she said she wouldn't change her exam style. You have to really put in the work to understand the content rather than just memorizing. Read the textbook and go to office hours for feedback

to the person who was complaining about the "generalized feedback" and wanting more specific feedback from her, you clearly don't take advantage of office hours. She will literally give you feedback on your assignments if you EMAIL HER AND GO TO OFFICE HOURS. it's not that hard. ya the class has a lot of reading and cases but you get cheat sheet

is this a crim class or bible study? If you don't know and memorize the textbook like the back of your hand, you are not going to do well. Hundreds of criminal cases in the textbook, and she will ask exam/assignment questions on any of them. Everything is based on the readings, she genuinely said we need to study it like the bible.
grading system is ridiculous, does not offer anything but "generalized feedback". you will have no idea what to improve on as she is too lazy to give you feedback. wouldn't recommend her at all.

Yes, she's tough. Actively engage with the material, do the readings, and you'll pass. Don't blame your poor grade on the prof if you didn't put in the effort that she tells you to put in. She is intense and her exams/assignments are challenging, but she certainly deserves better than two stars.

amy is pretty good, the class has a lot of reading but if you actually do it you'll be fine, I don't really like the semi online format but oh well. the class average on the midterm was bad but I (and her) think people probably didn't do the readings and just used chat gpt to make their cheat sheets. its hard but if you do the work it'll be okay

Amy is a pretty good prof. Yes her class is hard but she is very clear about what her expectations are and tells you how to do good in her class. There are 3 memos that you have to write and your worst grade is dropped. Midterm and Final were a bit tough but you get a cheat sheet which definitely help!

Does not explain at all, it is all textbook based and with crim230 material it is tough to understand just by reading a textbook. Seems to be annoyed when questions are asked and rushes through classes.

I took CRIM135 with Amy and decided to take 230 with her again. She teaches to the class instead of reading from the slides and she knows what she's talking about, making it easier to grasp. Don't cram this class and do go to lecture because some things she says only during lecture WILL be tested. If you put in the work, you'll do good.

Oh my god, Amy is the worst professor I have ever had. Her slides are unhelpful so you need to follow along with her voice, but then she speaks really fast, and she loses her train of thought and you have no idea what is going on anymore. She teaches from the textbook, so read it or you won't get anything, and its as if she wants you to fail!!!

so hard to do well in this class as instructions are vague and you never know what she actually wants. grading is so harsh. also, we do not get the assignments back to look at our individual feedback, but we get “general feedback” which is not helpful. almost seems like she wants us to do poorly.

The class is basically self-taught through the textbook. You dont get much out of lectures since they dont teach anything, and instead are short summaries of long readings. Instructions are vague, and youre left guessing what she actually wants. also it doesnt help that shes a tough grader - so take at your own risk!

Oh man, I already regret taking CRIM230 and it hasn't been a month. Take this course with anyone else, and you'll do fine. Extremely tough grader, instructions and expectations are never clear, lectures are practically useless and you're expected to teach yourself through the textbook.

Worst prof to ever exist. Take the class with someone else. She will never be happy with your work, even if you go above and beyond because she won't tell you what she wants. Unless ur a psychic that can predict what she wants, go with another prof.

Taking crim230 with her currently. Honestly, she seems sweet but her teaching style isn't the best. It's clear that we need to read everything in the textbook. Lecture slides aren't helpful at all. Seems like I need to teach myself this course because Conroy doesn't make sense.

Amy is clearly passionate about both the subject and teaching. Success is determined by keeping up w lecture and regularly reviewing as it is content heavy. I treated the course like a 100 level crim class and failed the midterm. After putting in a bit more effort and attending TA/prof office hours i was able to get a 91 on the final.
So difficult to pass

Would not recommend. She was never on time, and lectures weren't helpful at all. The entire course is taught by the textbook. Her voice is very high pitched yet hoarse, and VERY LOUD, making it hard to listen to lectures.

Amy has NEVER been on time for a single lecture, even for the midterm, which took more time away from the in-class assignments that were extremely rushed to begin with. She doesn't give clear instructions or examples of how she wants us to do things so good luck with the memos. Doesn't give specific feedback so you'll never know what you did wrong!

I have never disliked a professor as much as Amy Conroy in my 3 years at SFU. She has ruined my spark for crim, and the reason I switched out of the program. In lectures she just yaps about everything and nothing all at once. She makes no sense. She doesn't teach and expects us to read the entire textbook from front to back and learn ourselves.
According to Amy I should have started studying the material as soon as I exited the womb

Amy Conroy is the bane of my existence. Girlie just rambles on forever about how we need to know everything related to criminal law and then gives us maybe 20 minutes to scramble and write an in-class assignment worth 20% of our grade. Always shows up late and starts late too! I feel like I am a 7-year-old being lectured by my mom every time I go.

Amy has such a clear passion which made the difficult content easier to digest. Ive never done a rating here but I wanted to express how much she bettered my experience. Her slides dont include the full content, so going to class is very helpful (she does post audio recordings). She explains very clearly and has clear criteria for assignments.
I liked her. I think if you are engaged with her lectures they make sense and she explains things pretty well. The grading is tough in this class though for all of the assignments and exams.

Understand how ppl think shes a tough grader but I also think its because the course material is difficult in itself & requires students to put in the work. I enjoyed this class because of the interesting course content but the lectures are pretty useless, textbook is much more important. Shes very clear on what it takes to do well in her course

I don't know why there are so many negative comments about Amy. From the beginning she said we needed to read all the chapters in order to do good and she gave very clear instructions for the midterm and final. Don't hate on the prof if you are just not willing to read and to the bare minimum for a challenging class.

Amy sucks I genuinely had like 5 mental breakdowns over this class. Her lectures are just her ranting for 2 hours and her slides have no information on them. She tries to start discussions in lectures but nobody speaks because everyone is probably asleep from her tangents. All her content is heavily based on the textbook so make sure you read it

Amy made a 200 level course harder than it should have been. LOTS of readings, many real life cases from the textbook and she would not let us know which ones should be focused on. Slides were useless, lecture discussions often led to tangents.

I don't know why everyone seems to hate her. She is perfectly fine, good even. This course's assignments and exams were very reading focused, the lectures just highlighted things she was more likely to ask on exams etc. went into this course anxious because of the ratings, but it was perfectly fine. don't be scared. you'll do great.

Graeme is passionate about what he teaches and the concepts learned in class were interesting. 3 written assignments were tough but not impossible. Midterm and final were both open book but limited time for exams and he likes to do trick questions. Always recommend taking 135 or 230 with him. Great prof

Don't even bother with this professor. Just don't. Think you know criminal law? Think you will do fine? Think again. He has very high expectations, will not give you the rubric, and marks super hard. For a 200 level course, he makes it way too difficult. Save your gpa, save your mental it really is not worth it

insanely tough course for a 200 level. has insane expectations without giving the rubric. TA is actually crazy because they do not help, nor do they know what is going on; every mark you get back will BREAK you and your GPA. DO NOT TAKE HIM BECAUSE HE WILL FAIL YOU.

don't. take. this. guy. first assignment will BREAK you, they don't explain anything nor give u feedback. they don't even give you a RUBRIC. TA's are questionable and have no idea what to do, will teach u things verbally but not physically teach you how to format. midterm (no time, too strict, avg was 50-57% and said he was happy) 5/10. exam? hard.

Professor Bowbrick is intelligent and his lectures go through the criminal code well. However, it is very difficult to succeed in his course. He does not provide feedback and when he does, it is in person so you won't be able to record down any notes he has. His TAs are questionable, and you basically have to read his mind to get a good mark.

Graeme is not an easy prof, you have to work for a good grade in this course. That being said, I would 100% recommend taking any course you can with him. He is hilarious, and makes the material memorable and easy to understand. He expects a lot from his students but his passion for the material makes you interested in it. One of my favorite profs!

Honestly the most inspirational, as well as the hardest professor I've ever had. He really makes you work for your grade but it pays off and you WILL learn, and won't take anything half-assed. I'm glad I have the chance to have him again in 330 because I've honestly changed a lot of how I think about things because of him.

He's a great lecturer and definitely knows the law in and out; however, he expects a lot for a lower division class. He does not let you bring the pocket criminal code into exams (unlike other Crim 230 classes) and makes you memorize the codes. He also has somewhat of an attitude problem, where he almost seems confrontational in office hours.

Graeme is a very good lecturer. In addition to this, it is consistent that if you do take his class, you probably won't need the textbook, which is awesome as it is lecture heavy. However, I feel Graeme expects a heck of a lot more than the average prof at SFU. I deem this unfair for lower div classes as students will often take a huge GPA hit.

Loved this class. Graeme is a really great lecturer and the class was incredibly interesting.  Make sure to read the textbook before classes!  You definitely need to study for the tests (which are mostly writing-focused) but if you do then it'll be fine.  Participation in tutorials also mandatory but tutorials were also interesting.

He''s a really good professor if you''re there to learn the material. If you''re looking for an easy A though then he''s not the prof you''re looking for. His exams are very fair but he marks very hard on the fact patterns. Everything is lecture based so if you show up and take notes you''ll pass but he makes you REALLY work for an A.

I had the chance to have Rebecca as a professor for several classes and honestly she's one of the best teachers I ever had! She not only made the course material interesting and easy to understand, but she also went out of her way to help me and make sure I understood what was expected of me. Would definitely recommend taking her classes!!

Most of the profs I've had usually sick to lecturing the course material from the textbook. While Rebecca incorporates real life examples. Upon which is further analysed by classroom discussion. She is very approachable, marks fair, and provides feedback where she would like you to elaborate more. Would definitely recommend taking her class.

Pam is a great professor. One of the best in the criminology department. She understands the needs of her students and is available to help students with assignments and course materials. Pam has clear grading components and tells you what you need to know for the exam. Take every class you can with Pam.

Pamela is an amazing instructor. She is very passionate and very helpful. She is also super relatable and puts in a lot of effort to help students succeed and have an enjoyable time in the class. I would 100% take her again.
One of the best professors Ive had at SFU. Passionate about what she teaches. Shes understanding for student problems and has a very interesting lectures and assignments.

Pam must be the best professor I've met at SFU so far. She really puts lots of effort into teaching to make sure you understand the material and enjoy learning. She wants students to succeed and provides an engaging class. The assignments are fun and require deep thoughts and critical analysis; which is a chance to share your thoughts!
A nice person but the creative assignment was a bit vague on the criteria.

Pam is one of the most creative professors I have had the privilege of being taught by. Her lectures are designed in a way that relates and conforms to society currently. She is also very personable and understands the struggles that students go through. She also allows time for students to ask questions regarding exams, assignments, etc.

Pamla has been by far the most down to earth and caring prof I have ever had at SFU. She uses her own experiences as a student during her undergrad to teach better and to ensure students don't have the same miss-haps she did when she was in university. I would Strongly recommend Pamela as she is a gem that accurately relates to students today.
She literally makes learning about murderers fun and simple ahah. Loved the class :)

Pam is the best prof I have ever & will ever have! She is incredibly kind, caring, & reasonable. She creates an environment by how she teaches that engages with the class, which allows for the material to be understood easily, & it made the course incredibly enjoyable. She does not teach like it is simply her job but instead with true passion.

Pam is the best prof I've ever had. She gets to know her students and truly cares for them. Her assignments and response paper are fun and easier than research papers. She values students and their mental health and will happily help you if you reach out. She truly cares and I look forward to future classes she teaches.

Iryna is one of the most caring and passionate proffessors you can have. She challenges you in a very effective way to excercise critical thought and your own mind. She may be more difficult than some professors, but she compensates with willingness to help students. You will always get out the effort you put into her classes.

Criminal Law class is pretty hard, but Iryna really tries to help her students. Attend lectures, tutorials and office hours, read the textbook - and you will be fine. I promise that you won't regret taking this class with her!

Iryna is a very nice lady, but unfortunately her lectures left a bit to be desired. She spent about half of her lectures reading from her slides from the previous lecture which may sound helpful, but it proved to be otherwise. When she got into the new material it was rushed and not particularly well put together.

Helene was quite a hard marker, but overall a decently fair professor. LOTS of content. The time constraints for the exams were difficult, and the final was a bit ill-organized but she compensated fairly afterward. A harder class, but Helene as a professor was okay. Nothing to be afraid of, but definitely not an easy A class.

Took 135 with her in person and really struggled due to the amount of cases/statutes youre expected to remember, but 230 online was substantially easier since open book. If you have to take 135/230 with her, definitely take it remote or youll suffer trying to memorize the entire book

The course content itself is difficult (lots of reading!) but she explains things well. She is easy to talk to in office hours and gives good advice when its one-on-one. Not much time to complete online exams, but manageable if you dont fall behind and study hard.

I personally wouldn't want to take any classes with her because she's too dry in her lectures, but she's an alright prof. There's in class group discussion every week which is kinda awkward, but it helps us engage with other students and retain some materials. Make good use of the tutorial if you feel that you don't understand the lecture

I wish her slides were more detailed. The exam questions are difficult; even though questions did not explicitly state to, I did well by throwing down as much information I knew on simple questions because it was implicitly expected. Otherwise great prof, super passionate & caring - just be prepared to do a lot of heavy lifting in this course.

Easy to follow, likeable. Issues: doesn't allocate enough time to complete exams. Idk why she always references 'one minute per question' when half of her questions are written - expecting definitions, C.C. references, Statue references, opinion, likelihood of conviction, etc. It takes me over a minute just to read the question in front of me.
